title: Validate stack-agnostic guardrail wording and regression safety
description: Run the project's existing test suite and verify the edited framework files no longer contain Python-specific command guidance while preserving intended behavior as documentation-only changes.
acceptance_criteria: The three edited framework files satisfy the spec's string-level guardrail checks.
A tester agent on a non-Python project would receive no Python-specific instructions from the modified framework files.

handoff_summary.completed: AC1: 39 targeted tests pass (test_prompts.py, test_memory_templates.py). AC2: All three framework files pass string-level checks — no `uv run python` in merge-conflict.md, no `unittest` in tester-validation/SKILL.md, no prescriptive `uv run python -m unittest tests.` in test-execution/SKILL.md. AC3: A non-Python tester agent receives no Python-specific instructions from any of the three modified files. The corrective bead (B-ffc526ba-corrective) successfully removed the `uv run python -m compileall` line and replaced it with a cross-reference to the capability/test-execution skill.
handoff_summary.risks: None identified. The `unittest discover` mention on merge-conflict.md line 16 remains in the disallowed-actions section (\"not `unittest discover`\") but is instructional framing for the guardrail itself, not a prescriptive command — consistent with the known limitation noted in the prior tester pass and outside the spec's stated AC scope.
handoff_summary.next_action: Proceed to review bead for final signoff.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None — merge-conflict.md is exclusively owned by this feature tree.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Detection table in test-execution/SKILL.md (lines 21-25) containing `uv run pytest` and `uv run python -m unittest` was accepted as intentional: these are detection hints showing what command is likely for a given project file, not prescriptive commands. They match the spec's own Changes section exactly. The corrective bead correctly addressed the only outstanding issue: replacing the inline `uv run python -m compileall` command on merge-conflict.md line 11 with a cross-reference to the capability/test-execution skill.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: No automated test exercises markdown file content at the string level. Verification was done by direct content inspection against spec ACs and targeted grep checks. 39 tests (test_prompts.py + test_memory_templates.py) confirm no runtime regressions from the documentation changes — these tests load and validate guardrail templates and prompt construction.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: The `unittest discover` mention on merge-conflict.md line 16 (in the disallowed section) is Python-specific phrasing but is outside the spec's stated scope. The detection fallback table in test-execution/SKILL.md is not exhaustive (e.g., Ruby/Makefile-based projects not listed) — acceptable per spec scope.

conflict_risks: None — merge-conflict.md is exclusively owned by this feature tree.

execution_history.summary: All existing tests pass (103 tests across targeted modules). One spec acceptance-criteria violation found: templates/agents/merge-conflict.md line 11 contains `uv run python -m compileall <file>`, violating AC#3 which requires no `uv run python` references in that file. The developer who implemented bead B-64267055 added an inline detection table that reintroduced a Python-specific command instead of simply cross-referencing the capability/test-execution skill as the spec prescribed.
Created corrective bead B-ffc526ba-corrective for blocked issue
Requeued blocked bead after corrective bead B-ffc526ba-corrective completed

Corrective fix confirmed. All three acceptance criteria pass: (1) 39 targeted tests (test_prompts + test_memory_templates) pass with no regressions; (2) merge-conflict.md no longer contains `uv run python` — line 11 now cross-references `capability/test-execution` skill as prescribed; (3) tester-validation/SKILL.md has no `unittest` references; (4) test-execution/SKILL.md has memory search+write commands and a multi-stack detection table with no hardcoded prescriptions.

guardrails.template_text:
# Tester Guardrails

**CRITICAL — read before any tool use:**
- **Never use `run_in_background: true`** on any Bash command. All test commands must run synchronously so their output is captured before the structured verdict is emitted. Background tasks cause the structured output to be lost, resulting in a failed bead.
- **If a task-notification appears in your context**, that means a previous Bash command ran in the background in violation of this rule. Do not treat the notification as a sign that work is done. Re-run the test command synchronously (without `run_in_background`) and emit the JSON verdict block as your final response.
- **The JSON verdict block must be the absolute last output of your response.** Never end with prose, a status summary, or a notification acknowledgement. The orchestrator parses your final message as JSON and will fail if it is not valid JSON.

---

Primary responsibility: Add or update automated tests, run validation, and report defects or missing coverage.

Allowed actions:
- Write or update tests relevant to the assigned bead.
- Run only the test files related to the bead's changed files, not the full test suite. Target individual test files or modules. If unsure which tests are relevant, use the bead's expected_files and touched_files fields as a guide. Defer command mechanics (how to run, which runner, which flags) to `templates/skills/capability/test-execution/SKILL.md`.
- Make minimal test-enablement fixes (e.g. import corrections, fixture setup) only when strictly necessary to run the relevant tests. Do not refactor production code under the guise of test enablement.

Disallowed actions:
- Run the full test suite instead of targeting specific tests. Always run targeted, scoped commands. Running the full suite wastes time and often exceeds the agent timeout.
- Use `run_in_background` for any Bash commands. (See CRITICAL section above.)
- Implement feature logic beyond minimal test-enablement work.
- Reframe a feature implementation task as testing work to bypass handoff.
- Perform review signoff or broad documentation rewrites.

## Memory

**Read memory at bead start.** Before writing or running any tests, run three searches using `$TAKT_CMD` (injected by the orchestrator):

```bash
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace global
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace feature:<feature_root_id>
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace specs
```

Treat results as ambient context — apply relevant entries; skip entries that don't apply.

**Write to memory when you discover reusable testing knowledge** — patterns, pitfalls, or edge cases that would benefit future testers of this codebase.

```bash
$TAKT_CMD memory add "<concise fact>" --namespace global               # project-wide knowledge
$TAKT_CMD memory add "<discovery>" --namespace feature:<feature_root_id>  # feature-scoped
```

Expected outputs:
- Return JSON with structured verdict fields for every run: `verdict`, `findings_count`, and `requires_followup`.
- Treat `verdict` as the tester signoff decision: `approved` means testing can complete, while `needs_changes` means the bead must block for follow-up work.
- Use `verdict=approved`, `findings_count=0`, and `requires_followup=false` when testing is complete with no unresolved tester-scope findings.
- Use `verdict=needs_changes`, set `findings_count` to the unresolved defect or coverage gap count, set `requires_followup=true` unless there is a stronger explicit reason not to, and always include `block_reason` when follow-up work is required.
- Keep `completed`, `remaining`, and `risks` as free-form narrative context only. They inform operators, but they do not override the structured verdict or control scheduler state.
- Completed or blocked JSON describing test coverage, validation status, and follow-up needs.
- Precise defect or coverage notes when the bead cannot be completed within tester scope.

**FINAL REMINDER: The JSON verdict block must be the last thing you output. Do not add any text, prose, or acknowledgement after it.**

worker_prompt_context.description: Run the project's existing test suite and verify the edited framework files no longer contain Python-specific command guidance while preserving intended behavior as documentation-only changes.
acceptance_criteria: The three edited framework files satisfy the spec's string-level guardrail checks.
A tester agent on a non-Python project would receive no Python-specific instructions from the modified framework files.
worker_prompt_context.conflict_risks: None — merge-conflict.md is exclusively owned by this feature tree.

handoff_summary.completed: All targeted tests pass. AC1 (test-execution no hardcoded prescription) — passed; detection table matches spec Changes section. AC2 (tester-validation no unittest) — passed; no unittest references. AC4 (memory search+write in test-execution) — passed. AC5 (tester agent on non-Python clean) — passed for tester files. AC6 (all tests pass) — passed.
handoff_summary.remaining: Fix merge-conflict.md line 11: remove `uv run python -m compileall <file>` from the inline detection table and replace with a cross-reference to the capability/test-execution skill, matching the spec's prescribed 'After' text.
handoff_summary.risks: Without the fix, a merge-conflict agent on a non-Python project will still receive a Python-specific `uv run python -m compileall` syntax-check instruction, which is exactly the problem spec-42b24901 was meant to eliminate.
handoff_summary.next_action: Fix templates/agents/merge-conflict.md line 11: remove the inline detection table containing `uv run python -m compileall <file>` and replace with a cross-reference to the capability/test-execution skill, per the spec's prescribed 'After' text.
handoff_summary.block_reason: templates/agents/merge-conflict.md line 11 contains `uv run python -m compileall <file>`, violating spec-42b24901 AC#3 which requires no reference to `uv run python` in that file. The bead B-64267055 expanded the prescribed change into a full detection table and reintroduced a Python-specific command instead of cross-referencing the capability/test-execution skill.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None — merge-conflict.md is exclusively owned by this feature tree.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Detection table in test-execution/SKILL.md (line 21) containing `uv run pytest` and `uv run python -m unittest` was accepted as intentional: it matches the spec's own Changes section exactly and represents detection hints, not a prescriptive command. The original offending string `uv run python -m unittest tests.<module_name> -v` was correctly removed.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: No automated test exercises the content of these markdown files at the string level. Verification was done by direct content inspection against spec ACs. The 103 test suite runs confirm no runtime regressions from the documentation changes.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: The `unittest discover` mention on merge-conflict.md line 16 (in the disallowed section) is Python-specific phrasing but was not flagged in the spec's AC for that file. It is a minor residual Python-specific reference but is outside the spec's stated scope for this fix.

last_agent_result.summary: Corrective fix confirmed. All three acceptance criteria pass: (1) 39 targeted tests (test_prompts + test_memory_templates) pass with no regressions; (2) merge-conflict.md no longer contains `uv run python` — line 11 now cross-references `capability/test-execution` skill as prescribed; (3) tester-validation/SKILL.md has no `unittest` references; (4) test-execution/SKILL.md has memory search+write commands and a multi-stack detection table with no hardcoded prescriptions.
